source_policy: Current official Chilli Surfboards model descriptions and governed canonical profiles are authoritative. Public family reflects manufacturer design intent. Board DNA describes behaviour but does not determine identity.

models.manufacturer_intent: Resurrected Andy Irons performance design developed around a proven high-performance curve.
models.editorial_reason: Its competition lineage and performance-first design intent place it in Performance Shortboards, not Daily Drivers.

models.manufacturer_intent: Modern high-performance shortboard built for speed, precision, sensitivity and tight pocket surfing.
models.editorial_reason: Chilli explicitly defines it as a modern high-performance shortboard.

models.manufacturer_intent: Scaled Blueprint high-performance design for progressing and advanced junior surfers.
models.editorial_reason: The model retains the Blueprint's performance identity rather than becoming a separate family.

models.manufacturer_intent: Summer board designed around speed, paddle power and a generous sweet spot in smaller, weaker waves.
models.editorial_reason: Weak-wave performance is its core role.

models.manufacturer_intent: User-friendly performance board intended for everyday conditions from weak beach breaks to stronger point waves.
models.editorial_reason: Chilli explicitly calls it an essential daily driver with broad range.

models.manufacturer_intent: Versatile one-board travel option balancing paddle, glide and responsive performance in medium to larger surf.
models.editorial_reason: Its one-board versatility is primary; stronger-wave range is secondary rather than a dedicated step-up identity.

models.manufacturer_intent: Good-wave one-board quiver designed for hollow, heavier surf, with step-up paddle and shortboard performance.
models.editorial_reason: The board is explicitly designed around hollow and heavier waves and paddles like a step-up.

models.manufacturer_intent: Heavy-water gun developed for Western Australian outer reefs and Hawaii.
models.editorial_reason: Dedicated big-wave and heavy-water purpose is explicit.

models.manufacturer_intent: Lengthened, lower-railed step-up for long-period, punchy and heavy surf.
models.editorial_reason: Chilli explicitly identifies it as the heavy-water Step-Up version.

models.manufacturer_intent: Progressive performance mid-length retaining paddle and flow while increasing control in stronger surf.
models.editorial_reason: Chilli explicitly places it in its next-generation mid-length category.

models.manufacturer_intent: Fast, forgiving and reliable junior shortboard designed for intermediate to advanced groms progressing into high-performance models.
models.editorial_reason: This is a scaled performance shortboard, not a fish or twin-derived family.

models.manufacturer_intent: Well-rounded, user-friendly step-down shortboard balancing speed, control and forgiveness across varied conditions.
models.editorial_reason: Broad everyday usability is the primary role.

models.manufacturer_intent: Performance mid-length designed for easy paddling, wave catching and lower-effort surfing.
models.editorial_reason: Chilli explicitly calls it a performance mid-length.

models.manufacturer_intent: Shorter, more manoeuvrable Mid Strength derivative retaining strong paddle and mid-length flow.
models.editorial_reason: Compact sizing increases performance but does not change its mid-length identity.

models.manufacturer_intent: Small-wave progression board for groms and developing surfers, with enough performance for stronger riders to enjoy weak surf.
models.editorial_reason: Its primary role is accessible small-wave surfing, not softboard identity.

models.manufacturer_intent: User-friendly performance twin designed to increase wave count and cover two-to-six-foot surf across frontside and backside conditions.
models.editorial_reason: Its unusually broad range and ease make Daily Driver the clearer public role, with performance twin retained as subtype.

models.manufacturer_intent: Wide, low-rocker summer board built to create speed and fun in weak surf and flat sections.
models.editorial_reason: Small-wave speed generation is the core design purpose.

models.manufacturer_intent: Updated version of Chilli's best all-round surfboard with greater speed, control and versatility.
models.editorial_reason: All-round versatility is explicitly the primary role.

models.manufacturer_intent: Rare Bird EVO in Twin Tech construction, retaining the same all-round daily-driver design intent.
models.editorial_reason: Twin Tech is a construction designation and does not make the board a Mid Length.

models.manufacturer_intent: Forgiving performance driver refined with elite surfers for intermediate and advanced riders.
models.editorial_reason: High-performance shortboard intent remains primary despite accessibility.

models.manufacturer_intent: High-performance squash-tail shortboard with a generous sweet spot and broad condition range.
models.editorial_reason: Chilli explicitly identifies it as a high-performance shortboard.

models.manufacturer_intent: Scaled Shortie for lighter intermediate and advanced surfers seeking high-performance progression.
models.editorial_reason: It retains the Shortie's performance-first identity.

models.manufacturer_intent: Modern twin-fin fish combining a retro outline, paddle power, swallow tail speed and refined performance rails.
models.editorial_reason: Fish and traditional twin-fin identity are explicit in the design.

models.manufacturer_intent: Trusted, fast all-round shortboard designed to perform in below-average one-to-four-foot conditions.
models.editorial_reason: Its all-round role and added accessibility make it a small-wave Daily Driver rather than a pure groveller.
